contentBlocks.original:
भज गोविन्दं भज गोविन्दं
गोविन्दं भज मूढमते |
सम्प्राप्ते सन्निहिते काले
नहि नहि रक्षति डुकृञ्करणे ‖ 1 ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhaja govindaṃ bhaja govindaṃ
govindaṃ bhaja mūḍhamate |
samprāpte sannihite kāle
nahi nahi rakṣati ḍukṛñkaraṇe ‖ 1 ‖
contentBlocks.translation: Worship Govinda, worship Govinda, worship Govinda, O fool! The rules of grammar will not save you at the time of your death.
contentBlocks.meanings: भज (bhaja) – worship; गोविन्दं (govindaṃ) – Govinda; भज (bhaja) – worship; गोविन्दं (govindaṃ) – Govinda; गोविन्दं (govindaṃ) – Govinda; भज (bhaja) – worship; मूढमते (mūḍhamate) – O fool (mūḍha:foolish | mate:mind); सम्प्राप्ते (samprāpte) – having arrived; सन्निहिते (sannihite) – approaching; काले (kāle) – time; नहि (nahi) – not; नहि (nahi) – not; रक्षति (rakṣati) – protects; डुकृञ्करणे (ḍukṛñkaraṇe) – rules of grammar;

contentBlocks.original:
मूढ जहीहि धनागमतृष्णां
कुरु सद्बुद्धिम् मनसि वितृष्णाम् |
यल्लभसे निज कर्मोपात्तं
वित्तं तेन विनोदय चित्तम् ‖ 2 ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mūḍha jahīhi dhanāgamatṛṣṇāṃ
kuru sadbuddhim manasi vitṛṣṇām |
yallabhase nija karmopāttaṃ
vittaṃ tena vinodaya cittam ‖ 2 ‖
contentBlocks.translation: O fool! Give up your thirst to amass wealth, devote your mind to dispassion and thoughts of the Real. Be content with what comes to you through actions performed by your own work.
contentBlocks.meanings: मूढ (mūḍha) – O fool; जहीहि (jahīhi) – give up; धनागमतृष्णां (dhanāgamatṛṣṇām) – thirst for wealth (dhana:wealth | āgama:coming | tṛṣṇām:thirst); कुरु (kuru) – do; सद्बुद्धिम् (sadbuddhim) – good intellect (sat:good | buddhim:intellect); मनसि (manasi) – in the mind; वितृष्णाम् (vitṛṣṇām) – dispassion; यल्लभसे (yallabhase) – what you obtain; निज (nija) – own; कर्मोपात्तं (karmopāttaṃ) – obtained by actions (karma:actions | upāttaṃ:obtained); वित्तं (vittaṃ) – wealth; तेन (tena) – with that; विनोदय (vinodaya) – delight; चित्तम् (cittam) – mind;

contentBlocks.original:
यावद्-वित्तोपार्जन सक्तः
तावन्-निजपरिवारो रक्तः |
पश्चाज्जीवति जर्जर देहे
वार्तां कोऽपि न पृच्छति गेहे ‖ 5 ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yāvad-vittopārjana saktaḥ
tāvan-nijaparivāro raktaḥ |
paścājjīvati jarjara dehe
vārtāṃ ko'pi na pṛcchati gehe ‖ 5 ‖
contentBlocks.translation: So long as a man is fit and able to support his family by earning wealth, all those family members around him show affection. But no one at home cares for him, even have a word with him, when his body becomes invalid and totters due to old age.
contentBlocks.meanings: यावत् (yāvat) – as long as; वित्त (vitta) – wealth; उपार्जन (upārjana) – earning; सक्तः (saktaḥ) – attached; तावत् (tāvat) – so long; निज (nija) – own; परिवारः (parivāraḥ) – family; रक्तः (raktaḥ) – affectionate; पश्चात् (paścāt) – afterwards; जीवति (jīvati) – lives; जर्जर (jarjara) – decrepit; देहे (dehe) – in the body; वार्ताम् (vārtām) – news; कः (kaḥ) – who; अपि (api) – even; न (na) – not; पृच्छति (pṛcchati) – asks; गेहे (gehe) – at home;

contentBlocks.original:
यावत्-पवनो निवसति देहे
तावत्-पृच्छति कुशलं गेहे |
गतवति वायौ देहापाये
भार्या बिभ्यति तस्मिन् काये ‖ 6 ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yāvat-pavano nivasati dehe
tāvat-pṛcchati kuśalaṃ gehe |
gatavati vāyau dehāpāye
bhāryā bibhyati tasmin kāye ‖ 6 ‖
contentBlocks.translation: When one is alive, his family members enquire kindly about his welfare. But when the life-air stops and the soul departs from the body, even his wife runs away in fear of the corpse
contentBlocks.meanings: यावत् (yāvat) – as long as; पवनः (pavanaḥ) – life-air; निवसति (nivasati) – resides; देहे (dehe) – in the body; तावत् (tāvat) – so long; पृच्छति (pṛcchati) – asks; कुशलम् (kuśalam) – welfare; गेहे (gehe) – in the house; गतवति (gatavati) – when departed; वायौ (vāyau) – life-air; देहापाये (dehāpāye) – at body's end (deha:body | apāye:end); भार्या (bhāryā) – wife; बिभ्यति (bibhyati) – fears; तस्मिन् (tasmin) – in that; काये (kāye) – body;

contentBlocks.original:
मा कुरु धनजन यौवन गर्वं
हरति निमेषात्-कालः सर्वम् |
मायामयमिदम्-अखिलं हित्वा
ब्रह्मपदं त्वं प्रविश विदित्वा ‖ 11 ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mā kuru dhanajana yauvana garvaṃ
harati nimeṣāt-kālaḥ sarvam |
māyāmayamidam-akhilaṃ hitvā
brahmapadaṃ tvaṃ praviśa viditvā ‖ 11 ‖
contentBlocks.translation: Do not take pride in wealth, friends and youth. Each one of these is destroyed within an instant by Time. Free yourself from the illusion of the world of māyā and attain the realm of brahman, timeless truth.
contentBlocks.meanings: मा (mā) – do not; कुरु (kuru) – do; धनजन (dhanajana) – wealth and people (dhana:wealth | jana:people); यौवन (yauvana) – youth; गर्वं (garvaṃ) – pride; हरति (harati) – takes away; निमेषात् (nimeṣāt) – in an instant; कालः (kālaḥ) – time; सर्वम् (sarvam) – everything; मायामयम् (māyāmayam) – illusory (māyā:illusion | maya:made of); इदम् (idam) – this; अखिलं (akhilaṃ) – entire; हित्वा (hitvā) – abandoning; ब्रह्मपदं (brahmapadaṃ) – realm of brahman (brahma:brahman | padaṃ:realm); त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; प्रविश (praviśa) – enter; विदित्वा (viditvā) – knowing;

contentBlocks.original:
सुरमन्दिर तरु मूल निवासः
शय्या भूतलम्-अजिनं वासः |
सर्व परिग्रह भोगत्यागः
कस्य सुखं न करोति विरागः ‖ 19 ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suramandira taru mūla nivāsaḥ
śayyā bhūtalam-ajinaṃ vāsaḥ |
sarva parigraha bhogatyāgaḥ
kasya sukhaṃ na karoti virāgaḥ ‖ 19 ‖
contentBlocks.translation: One who lives in temples or dwells at the foot of trees, whose bed is the surface of the earth, whose garment is a deer-skin, who has thus renounced all enjoyment of worldly possessions - to whom will such dispassion (vairāgya) not bring happiness? (Stanza attributed to Nityānanda.)
contentBlocks.meanings: सुरमन्दिर (suramandira) – temple; तरु (taru) – tree; मूल (mūla) – root; निवासः (nivāsaḥ) – dwelling; शय्या (śayyā) – bed; भूतलम् (bhūtalam) – surface of the earth; अजिनं (ajinaṃ) – deer-skin; वासः (vāsaḥ) – garment; सर्व (sarva) – all; परिग्रह (parigraha) – possessions; भोगत्यागः (bhogatyāgaḥ) – renunciation of enjoyment (bhoga:enjoyment | tyāgaḥ:renunciation); कस्य (kasya) – to whom; सुखं (sukhaṃ) – happiness; न (na) – not; करोति (karoti) – does; विरागः (virāgaḥ) – dispassion;

contentBlocks.original:
भगवद्गीता किञ्चिदधीता
गङ्गा जललव कणिका पीता |
सकृदपि येन मुरारी समर्चा
क्रियते तस्य यमेन न चर्चा ‖ 21 ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhagavadgītā kiñcidadhītā
gaṅgā jalalava kaṇikā pītā |
sakṛdapi yena murārī samarcā
kriyate tasya yamena na carcā ‖ 21 ‖
contentBlocks.translation: Let a man read but a little from Bhagavad-gītā, drink just a drop of Gaṅgā-water, worship but once murāri, the enemy of 'Murā' (Lord Kṛṣṇa); he then will have no confrontation with Yama, the Lord of death. (Stanza attributed to Dṛḍhabhakta.)
contentBlocks.meanings: भगवद्गीता (bhagavadgītā) – Bhagavad Gita (bhagavat:divine | gītā:song); किञ्चित् (kiñcit) – a little; अधीता (adhītā) – studied; गङ्गा (gaṅgā) – Ganga; जललव (jalalava) – drop of water (jala:water | lava:drop); कणिका (kaṇikā) – particle; पीता (pītā) – drunk; सकृत् (sakṛt) – once; अपि (api) – even; येन (yena) – by whom; मुरारी (murārī) – Murari (Krishna); समर्चा (samarcā) – worshipped; क्रियते (kriyate) – is done; तस्य (tasya) – his; यमेन (yamena) – with Yama; न (na) – not; चर्चा (carcā) – discussion;

contentBlocks.original:
पुनरपि जननं पुनरपि मरणं
पुनरपि जननी जठरे शयनम् |
इह संसारे बहु दुस्तारे
कृपयाऽपारे पाहि मुरारे ‖ 22 ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
punarapi jananaṃ punarapi maraṇaṃ
punarapi jananī jaṭhare śayanam |
iha saṃsāre bahu dustāre
kṛpayā'pāre pāhi murāre ‖ 22 ‖
contentBlocks.translation: Birth again, death again, again resting in the mother's womb! It is indeed hard to cross this boundless ocean of saṁsāra (cycle of repeated birth and death). O Murāri! by your causeless mercy please protect me (from this transmigratory process). (Stanza attributed to Nityanātha.)
contentBlocks.meanings: पुनरपि (punarapi) – again (punar:again | api:also); जननं (jananam) – birth; पुनरपि (punarapi) – again (punar:again | api:also); मरणं (maraṇam) – death; पुनरपि (punarapi) – again (punar:again | api:also); जननी (jananī) – mother; जठरे (jaṭhare) – in the womb; शयनम् (śayanam) – resting; इह (iha) – here; संसारे (saṃsāre) – in the cycle of birth and death (saṃsāra:cycle of birth and death | e:in); बहु (bahu) – much; दुस्तारे (dustāre) – difficult to cross (dus:difficult | tāre:to cross); कृपया (kṛpayā) – by mercy; अपारे (apāre) – boundless; पाहि (pāhi) – protect; मुरारे (murāre) – O Murari;

contentBlocks.original:
गेयं गीता नाम सहस्रं
ध्येयं श्रीपति रूपम्-अजस्रम् |
नेयं सज्जन सङ्गे चित्तं
देयं दीनजनाय च वित्तम् ‖ 28 ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
geyaṃ gītā nāma sahasraṃ
dhyeyaṃ śrīpati rūpam-ajasram |
neyaṃ sajjana saṅge cittaṃ
deyaṃ dīnajanāya ca vittam ‖ 28 ‖
contentBlocks.translation: Regularly recite from the Gītā, meditate on Viṣṇu (śrīpati) in your heart, and chant his thousand glories names (viṣṇu-sahasranāma). Take delight to be with the noble and the holy. Distribute your wealth in charity to the poor and the needy. (Stanza attributed to Sumatir.)
contentBlocks.meanings: गेयम् (geyam) – to be sung; गीता (gītā) – Gītā; नाम (nāma) – name; सहस्रं (sahasraṃ) – thousand; ध्येयम् (dhyeyam) – to be meditated upon; श्रीपति (śrīpati) – Lord Viṣṇu (śrī:prosperity | pati:lord); रूपम् (rūpam) – form; अजस्रम् (ajasram) – constantly; नेयम् (neyam) – to be led; सज्जन (sajjana) – noble people (sat:good | jana:people); सङ्गे (saṅge) – in company; चित्तं (cittaṃ) – mind; देयम् (deyam) – to be given; दीनजनाय (dīnajanāya) – to the poor (dīna:poor | jana:people); च (ca) – and; वित्तम् (vittam) – wealth;

contentBlocks.original:
अर्थमनर्थं भावय नित्यं
नास्ति ततः सुख लेशः सत्यम् |
पुत्रादपि धनभाजां भीतिः
सर्वत्रैषा विहिता रीतिः ‖ 30 ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
arthamanarthaṃ bhāvaya nityaṃ
nāsti tataḥ sukha leśaḥ satyam |
putrādapi dhanabhājāṃ bhītiḥ
sarvatraiṣā vihitā rītiḥ ‖ 30 ‖
contentBlocks.translation: Remember always that wealth is the source of misfortune. The truth is that one cannot extract even a bit of happiness from it. For the rich, there is fear even from one’s own son. This is the established way with wealth everywhere.
contentBlocks.meanings: अर्थम् (artham) – wealth; अनर्थम् (anartham) – misfortune; भावय (bhāvaya) – consider; नित्यम् (nityam) – always; न (na) – not; अस्ति (asti) – is; ततः (tataḥ) – from it; सुख (sukha) – happiness; लेशः (leśaḥ) – bit; सत्यम् (satyam) – truth; पुत्रात् (putrāt) – from son; अपि (api) – even; धनभाजाम् (dhanabhājām) – of the rich (dhana:wealth | bhājām:of the possessors); भीतिः (bhītiḥ) – fear; सर्वत्र (sarvatra) – everywhere; एषा (eṣā) – this; विहिता (vihitā) – established; रीतिः (rītiḥ) – way;

contentBlocks.original:
गुरु चरणाम्भुज निर्भरभक्तः
संसाराद्-अचिराद्-भव मुक्तः |
सेन्द्रिय मानस नियमादेवं
द्रक्ष्यसि निज हृदयस्थं देवम् ‖ 32 ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
guru caraṇāmbhuja nirbharabhaktaḥ
saṃsārād-acirād-bhava muktaḥ |
sendriya mānasa niyamādevaṃ
drakṣyasi nija hṛdayasthaṃ devam ‖ 32 ‖
contentBlocks.translation: Oh devotee sincerely dedicated to the lotus feet of the Guru! May thou be soon free from Saṁsārā, the circle of birth and death. Through disciplined senses and controlled mind, thou shalt come to see (experience) the in-dwelling Lord of your heart.
contentBlocks.meanings: गुरु (guru) – teacher; चरणाम्भुज (caraṇāmbhuja) – lotus feet (caraṇa:feet | ambhuja:lotus); निर्भरभक्तः (nirbharabhaktaḥ) – sincerely dedicated devotee (nirbhara:sincerely | bhaktaḥ:devotee); संसाराद् (saṃsārād) – from Saṁsāra; अचिराद् (acirād) – soon; भव (bhava) – become; मुक्तः (muktaḥ) – free; सेन्द्रिय (sendriya) – with senses; मानस (mānasa) – mind; नियमादेवं (niyamādevaṃ) – through disciplined control (niyama:discipline | evaṃ:thus); द्रक्ष्यसि (drakṣyasi) – you shall see; निज (nija) – own; हृदयस्थं (hṛdayasthaṃ) – in-dwelling in heart (hṛdaya:heart | sthaṃ:dwelling); देवम् (devam) – Lord;
